pi_items.source: Checked setup_task.sh which generates the briefing document specifying 'Analyst Role: Anticipatory Action Climatologist'. Also present in verifier.py docstring.
Checked setup_task.sh which generates the briefing document for 'INTERNATIONAL FEDERATION OF RED CROSS AND RED CRESCENT SOCIETIES (IFRC)'. Also present in verifier.py docstring.
Verified via setup_task.sh (requires these exact paths) and install_panoply.sh which downloads real-world NCEP Reanalysis datasets to these locations.
Verified via setup_task.sh and export_result.sh which explicitly expect outputs to be saved to this directory.
Verified via export_result.sh which checks for these exact file names and paths to compute sizes, modification times, and extract text data for the verifier.

privileged_info_summary: The task relies on real NCEP Reanalysis monthly long-term mean datasets (precipitation rate and surface air temperature) downloaded directly from NOAA PSL. Python netCDF4 analysis confirms that for the target central Mongolian Steppe region (47.0°N, 105.0°E), the peak pasture month for precipitation is July (approximately 1.5e-5 kg/m²/s via nearest/interpolated reading) and the coldest winter month is January (with a mean temperature of approximately -18°C to -19°C). The verifier accurately scores values falling within physically plausible ranges ([1e-5, 5e-5] for precipitation, [-35, -15] for temperature). All expected output file paths and report configurations rigorously align with the setup and export scripts.
